markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close ED-REC-001 (+ MT-159 descoped half): editor-side crash/draft recovery - unsaved keystrokes survive a tab/app crash via periodic draft persistence to the backend write-box (NOT localStorage-only); on reopen, a recovery banner offers diff (reuse MT-247) + restore/discard; draft cleared on successful save. Closes the loop with backend MT-159 promoted-recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
Draft persistence uses the existing write-box/CRDT surfaces; recovery NEVER auto-overwrites the saved head without explicit choice.
scope.expected_behavior: Pull the plug mid-typing; reopen; Handshake offers the unsaved draft with a diff; restore brings every keystroke back.
acceptance_criteria: Simulated crash (kill page mid-edit) -> reopen -> draft offered + restored, byte-exact (Playwright).
Decline-restore leaves saved head untouched (negative test).
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils (scoped) green with real-PG tests for backend state.
pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence.summary: Implemented MT-255 backend-backed RichDocument draft recovery. Unsaved editor changes debounce to a PostgreSQL draft slot, reopen offers a recovery banner with MT-247 merge/diff reuse, restore rehydrates the editor byte-exact for the typed draft text, explicit discard deletes only the draft, and successful save clears the draft server-side.

verification.details: 27 tests passed, including MT-255 debounce persistence, backend recovery restore, save-clear UI, and discard-without-save cases.
1 real-PG route test passed. It proves draft upsert/get, EventLedger receipt ids for save/discard, save clears draft, discard leaves saved head untouched.

1 Chromium offline dependency-policy test passed. It built editor-draft-recovery.html and proved crash/reopen offer, restore exact typed draft text, save clears draft, and discard leaves saved head untouched with no external requests.
No whitespace errors; Git reported existing LF-to-CRLF warnings.
No whitespace errors; Git reported existing LF-to-CRLF warnings.

eventledger_postgres_evidence: Migration 0328 adds knowledge_rich_document_drafts keyed by rich_document_id with base version/hash, draft JSON/hash, actor/session attribution, and timestamps.
save_knowledge_rich_document_version deletes knowledge_rich_document_drafts in the same transaction as the promoted save.

DIFF_ATTACK_SURFACES: Discard accidentally overwrites or mutates saved head.
Malformed or unsafe draft content bypasses save validation.
Debounced draft write races with clean save.
CRDT update log alone lacks an explicit clear/consume operation.
INDEPENDENT_CHECKS_RUN: Frontend unit test validates debounced backend draft writes and restore/discard UI behavior.
Real-PG backend route test validates durable draft slot and receipts.
Offline Playwright proof validates crash/reopen through built UI.
COUNTERFACTUAL_CHECKS: If saved content equals draft content, GET /draft suppresses the recovery offer.
If the draft is explicitly discarded, saveRichDocument is not called and the editor remains on saved head.
If save succeeds, the draft row is deleted server-side, not merely hidden in UI.
BOUNDARY_PROBES: Draft PUT requires DocumentAction::Write and GET requires DocumentAction::Read.
Draft content is parsed through BlockTree and validate_block_embeds before persistence.
Base version newer than saved head and same-version hash mismatch fail closed with conflict.
NEGATIVE_PATH_CHECKS: Decline/discard recovery leaves saved head untouched in backend route test and Playwright proof.
Pending draft debounce is cancelled at save start to avoid stale post-save draft writes.
FINDINGS_FIXED: Rejected CRDT-log-only approach because it had no clear-on-save primitive; implemented a narrow PostgreSQL draft slot with explicit clear/discard.
Initial frontend discard assertion checked the wrong fixture text; fixed to assert no draft content was applied and no save occurred.
RESIDUAL_UNCERTAINTY: Playwright proof uses route-mocked loopback backend for the browser flow; real PostgreSQL/EventLedger persistence is covered by the route-level backend test.
Full packaged Tauri runtime was not launched for this MT.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
